factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across segments, with some showing growth and others facing challenges.

evidence.text: The company's revenue growth in the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment was driven by increased project execution activities, particularly in power projects, while the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment faced revenue declines due to reduced project execution activities in mining and metals.

factors.summary: Backlog orders increased overall, driven by new awards in certain segments, though some segments saw a decrease.

evidence.text: Consolidated backlog as of June 30, 2016 was $47.3 billion compared to $41.6 billion as of June 30, 2015. The increase in backlog was primarily due to new awards booked after the second quarter of 2015 in the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment.

factors.summary: The company is not explicitly mentioned to be raising prices, holding, or discounting, but pricing pressures are evident in certain segments.

evidence.text: The company is facing intense competition in the global engineering, procurement, and construction industry, which can place downward pressure on contract prices and profit margins.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment, which saw significant growth.

evidence.text: Revenue for the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment increased by 74 percent and 63 percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016, respectively, primarily due to increased project execution activities in the power business line.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating, particularly in the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment, which saw a 24% and 21% decrease in revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016, respectively.

evidence.text: Revenue for the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment decreased by 24 percent and 21 percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016, respectively, primarily due to a significant decline in volume of the mining and metals business line.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with some segments experiencing growth while others face declines. The Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment saw a decline in volumes, while the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment saw growth.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016 decreased by 24 percent and 21 percent, respectively, compared to the corresponding periods in 2015, primarily due to a significant decline in volume of the mining and metals business line.

factors.summary: End market momentum varies, with the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment showing strong growth, while the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment faces challenges due to reduced project execution activities and lower oil prices.

evidence.text: The Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment includes the operations of NuScale, which are primarily research and development activities. In May 2014, NuScale entered into a Cooperative Agreement establishing the terms and conditions of a funding award totaling $217 million under the DOE’s Small Modular Reactor Licensing Technical Support Program.

factors.summary: Input costs are not explicitly addressed in the provided text.

evidence.text: The text does not provide specific information on raw material, commodity, or component cost trends.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with the company facing some margin pressure from higher corporate general and administrative expenses, particularly related to the Stork acquisition.

evidence.text: Higher corporate general and administrative expenses, primarily associated with the Stork acquisition, affected net earnings. The company is managing these costs as part of its overall strategy.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable despite margin pressure from the Stork acquisition.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are under pressure, particularly in the Energy, Chemicals & Mining segment, where margins have decreased due to reduced revenue and project execution challenges.

evidence.text: Segment profit margin for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016 was 5.1 percent and 6.3 percent, respectively, compared to 7.0 percent and 7.2 percent for the corresponding periods in 2015. The decrease in segment profit margin during both periods was attributable to the same factors affecting revenue and segment profit.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are under pressure due to reduced revenue and project execution challenges.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction and efficiency programs, particularly in the Government segment, which has seen improvements in segment profit margins due to cost optimization efforts.

evidence.text: Segment profit for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016 increased 28 percent and 22 percent, respectively, compared to the corresponding periods in 2015. Segment profit increases during both periods resulted primarily from the continued implementation of the company’s cost optimization efforts as well as increased contributions from construction projects within the services business line.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully implementing cost reduction programs, leading to improved segment profit margins.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are evident, particularly in the Government segment, where cost optimization efforts have positively impacted segment profit margins.

evidence.text: Segment profit margin for the Government segment increased to 3.3 percent and 2.9 percent for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016, respectively, compared to 2.9 percent and 2.6 percent for the corresponding prior year periods. These increases were substantially due to the factors noted above that affected revenue and segment profit.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements have positively impacted segment profit margins in the Government segment.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are stable, with the company making strategic investments in partnerships and joint ventures, such as the CFHI joint venture, to support project execution activities.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures of $107 million and $133 million for the six months ended June 30, 2016 and 2015, respectively, primarily related to construction equipment associated with equipment operations in the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment, as well as expenditures for facilities and investments in information technology.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are stable, with strategic investments supporting project execution, but no clear positive or negative trend.

factors.summary: The company has a strong balance sheet with access to capital markets, and its debt leverage is manageable, as evidenced by its strong credit ratings and ability to refinance at competitive rates.

evidence.text: The company has committed and uncommitted lines of credit totaling $6.0 billion, which may be used for revolving loans and letters of credit. The company believes that for at least the next 12 months, cash generated from operations, along with its unused credit capacity of $4.0 billion and substantial cash position, is sufficient to support operating requirements.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong balance sheet and manageable debt leverage, supported by strong credit ratings and access to capital markets.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining its shareholder returns through consistent dividend payments and a stock repurchase program, signaling confidence in its future growth.

evidence.text: Quarterly cash dividends are typically paid during the month following the quarter in which they are declared. Therefore, dividends declared in the fourth quarter of 2015 were paid in the first quarter of 2016. Quarterly cash dividends of $0.21 per share were declared in the second quarter of 2016 and 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Consistent dividend payments and stock repurchase program indicate confidence in future growth.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation has decreased compared to prior periods, primarily due to increased working capital requirements associated with project execution activities.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $91 million for the six months ended June 30, 2016 compared to $204 million for the corresponding period of the prior year. The decrease in free cash generated from operating activities resulted primarily from a significantly larger increase in working capital in 2016 compared to 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in free cash flow due to increased working capital requirements associated with project execution.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is creating value, particularly with the Stork acquisition, which has contributed to backlog growth and segment expansion.

evidence.text: On March 1, 2016, the company acquired 100 percent of Stork Holding B.V. for an aggregate purchase price of 695 million (or approximately $756 million), including the assumption of debt and other liabilities. The operations of Stork are reported in the Maintenance, Modification & Asset Integrity segment below.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisition activity, particularly the Stork acquisition, is contributing to backlog growth and segment expansion, indicating value creation.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to the mention of various risks and uncertainties, including economic conditions, competition, and regulatory factors, which could impact future results.

evidence.text: The company wishes to caution readers that forward-looking statements are subject to various risks and uncertainties which could cause actual results of operations to differ materially from expectations. Factors potentially contributing to such differences include intense competition, economic conditions affecting clients, and other market risks.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses caution due to multiple risks and uncertainties that could negatively impact future results.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include reduced demand in mining and metals, execution challenges on contracts, and economic uncertainties affecting clients and partners.

evidence.text: Factors potentially contributing to such differences include, among others: The cyclical nature of many of the markets the company serves, including our commodity-based business lines, and our vulnerability to downturns; Difficulties or delays incurred in the execution of contracts, or failure to accurately estimate the resources and time necessary for our contracts, resulting in cost overruns or liabilities; Current economic conditions affecting our clients, partners, subcontractors and suppliers, which may result in decreased capital investment or expenditures, or a failure to make anticipated increased capital investment or expenditures, by the company’s clients or other financial difficulties by our partners, subcontractors or suppliers.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include reduced demand in mining and metals, execution challenges, and economic uncertainties affecting clients and partners.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is focused on expanding in higher-growth segments, such as the Industrial, Infrastructure & Power segment, and investing in joint ventures to support global project execution.

evidence.text: The company is making strategic investments in partnerships and joint ventures, such as the CFHI joint venture, to support project execution activities and expand its capabilities in key markets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is focusing on expanding in higher-growth segments and investing in joint ventures to support global project execution, indicating a strategic growth plan.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, economic conditions affecting clients, competition, and currency fluctuations, which could significantly impact near-term results.

evidence.text: Factors potentially contributing to such differences include, among others: The cyclical nature of many of the markets the company serves, including our commodity-based business lines, and our vulnerability to downturns; Current economic conditions affecting our clients, partners, subcontractors and suppliers, which may result in decreased capital investment or expenditures, or a failure to make anticipated increased capital investment or expenditures, by the company’s clients or other financial difficulties by our partners, subcontractors or suppliers; Intense competition in the global engineering, procurement and construction industry, which can place downward pressure on our contract prices and profit margins; Currency risk and foreign exchange risks.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, economic conditions, competition, and currency fluctuations, which pose risks to near-term results.

factors.summary: Catalysts include new project awards, successful execution of contracts, and integration of acquired businesses like Stork, which are expected to drive future growth.

evidence.text: Consolidated new awards were $6.4 billion and $11.1 billion for the three and six months ended June 30, 2016, respectively, compared to new awards of $4.3 billion and $8.7 billion for the three and six months ended June 30, 2015. The Industrial, Infrastructure & Power and Government segments were the major contributors to the new award activity in the first half of 2016.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New project awards and successful contract execution, including integration of Stork, are expected to drive future growth.

factors.summary: The company has not provided any updated forward guidance for revenue, earnings, or margins in the filing. There are no explicit changes or statements regarding future financial performance.

evidence.text: The text does not contain explicit forward guidance regarding revenue, earnings, or margins.
There have been no material changes from our risk factors as disclosed in our Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2015.
The text does not provide specific forward guidance on revenue, earnings, or margins for future periods.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company has not provided updated forward guidance, resulting in no clear directional signal for future financial performance.
